claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and your children.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children that you'd like me to assess for Child Benefit eligibility?** If so, could you please tell me their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex and he's 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have one child, Alex, who is 17 years old.

Before I continue with more questions, let me check the eligibility rules to ensure I'm asking the right questions.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, I've reviewed the eligibility rules. I can see that since Alex is 17 years old, there are specific conditions that apply. Let me ask you the next important question:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** For example, is he at school, sixth form, college, or on an approved apprenticeship or training scheme?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education. He's studying for his A-levels at sixth form.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful - Alex is in approved education, which is positive for eligibility.

Now I need to ask a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Do you live with Alex, or does he live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's good to know.

Now, I have one more important question:

**Are you currently responsible for Alex's upkeep - meaning you're providing for his needs such as food, clothes, and accommodation?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I provide for all his needs - food, clothes, accommodation, everything he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for that information. I now have all the details I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me review the rules based on what you've told me:

- Alex is 17 years old
- He is in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form)
- He lives with you at home
- You are responsible for his upkeep and provide for all his needs

According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify if you're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK. However, for children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training.

Since Alex is 17, still in approved education (A-levels), living with you, and you're providing for his upkeep, you meet all the criteria for eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's the summary:

✓ **Alex is eligible for Child Benefit** because:
- He is 17 years old (under 20)
- He is in approved education (A-levels at sixth form)
- You live with him and are responsible for his upkeep
- You provide for all his needs (food, clothes, accommodation, etc.)

The key rule that applies here is that you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Since Alex meets all these criteria, you can claim Child Benefit for him.

If you'd like to proceed with a claim, you can contact HMRC or apply through the appropriate channels. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is currently in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form). According to Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex meets this criterion. Additionally, Alex lives with you at home and you are responsible for his upkeep by providing food, clothes, accommodation, and all his needs. You are therefore eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their child Alex. Alex is 17 years old and remains in approved education (A-levels at sixth form), which qualifies him for Child Benefit eligibility as he is under 20 and in approved education. The claimant lives with Alex and provides for all his needs, meeting the responsibility requirements for Child Benefit."]
